title: Digital Connection vs. Mental Well-being
content: This slide presents the topic of digital connection and its impact on mental well-being. It sets the stage for exploring how online interactions affect individuals psychologically.

title: The Ubiquitous Landscape
content: Gen Z are considered digital natives, experiencing life largely online. A large portion of students use YouTube and social media platforms like Instagram and Snapchat regularly, highlighting the indispensability of constant connectivity today.

title: Usage and Anxiety Statistics
content: A significant 87% of individuals use digital devices for more than two hours a day. There is a notable percentage of users reporting feelings of anxiety or sadness, linked to an increased risk of depression.

title: Psychological Manifestations
content: Digital connections lead to psychological effects like the FOMO effect, comparison issues, and cyberbullying. These phenomena contribute to stress, negative self-image, and widespread online harassment.

title: Strategic Mitigation Efforts
content: Strategies such as digital detox, awareness about platform algorithms, and platform responsibility are recommended. These strategies aim to balance emotional well-being and ensure user safety online.
key_claims: Digital detox is crucial for emotional balance
